name: OpenAI: GPT-5.5
description: OpenAI's current flagship. A reasoning-first generalist with a one-million-token window, strong tool use, and structured outputs. The default pick when you want the best OpenAI has without thinking about it.

name: OpenAI: GPT-5.5 Pro
description: The slow, expensive, maximum-effort tier of GPT-5.5. Same window and toolset, but the model spends far more compute per answer. Meant for hard one-shot problems, not chat traffic.

name: OpenAI: GPT-5.4
description: The previous OpenAI flagship, still widely deployed. Solid reasoning and tool calling at a quarter of the 5.5 price, with the same one-million-token window.

name: OpenAI: GPT-5.4 Mini
description: A small, fast member of the GPT-5.4 family. Keeps reasoning and structured outputs but trades depth for speed and price. Good default for high-volume product features.

name: OpenAI: GPT-5.4 Nano
description: The cheapest GPT-5.4 tier. Built for classification, extraction, and short answers at bulk prices where latency matters more than depth.

name: OpenAI: GPT-5.3-Codex
description: A GPT-5.3 variant tuned for software engineering: repository-scale edits, long agentic coding sessions, and careful diff-following. Prefers doing the task over talking about it.

name: OpenAI: GPT-4o
description: OpenAI's 2024 multimodal workhorse. Text and image input with dependable tool calling. Superseded at the frontier but stable, well understood, and broadly compatible.

name: OpenAI: GPT-4o-mini
description: The small sibling of GPT-4o and a long-time favorite for cheap pipelines. Fast, tool-capable, and priced for volume.

name: Anthropic: Claude Sonnet 5
description: Anthropic's mainline model. Frontier-level coding and agentic work with a one-million-token window at mid-tier pricing. Extended thinking is available on request.

name: Anthropic: Claude Opus 4.8
description: Anthropic's deepest reasoner. Slower and pricier than Sonnet, chosen when answer quality on hard problems is worth the wait. Strong on long autonomous coding runs.

name: Anthropic: Claude Sonnet 4.6
description: The previous Sonnet generation. Still a very capable coder and agent, kept around for teams pinned to its exact behavior.

name: Anthropic: Claude Haiku 4.5
description: Anthropic's fast tier. Near-Sonnet quality on everyday tasks at a fraction of the price and latency, with vision and tool use included.

name: Google: Gemini 3.5 Flash
description: Google's high-throughput generalist. Text, image, audio, and video input over a million-token window, with reasoning on tap. The price-performance pick of the Gemini line.

name: Google: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
description: The large Gemini, in preview. Deep multimodal reasoning over huge contexts; the choice when Flash isn't enough and cost is secondary.

name: Google: Gemini 3 Flash Preview
description: Preview build of the Gemini 3 Flash line. Fast multimodal inference at low cost, useful for testing against the newer stable Flash releases.

name: Google: Gemini 2.5 Flash
description: The 2025 Flash generation. Cheap, quick, fully multimodal, and battle-tested in production; a safe floor for cost-sensitive workloads.

name: DeepSeek: DeepSeek V4 Pro
description: DeepSeek's flagship MoE. Frontier-adjacent reasoning and coding at prices an order of magnitude below the big labs, with a million-token window and huge output budgets.

name: DeepSeek: DeepSeek V4 Flash
description: The fast V4 tier. Aggressively priced for bulk generation and simple agent steps while keeping the V4 family's tool calling.

name: DeepSeek: DeepSeek V3.2
description: The V3.2 sparse-attention release that first made long context cheap. Still a strong value pick for reasoning-light workloads.

name: DeepSeek: R1 0528
description: The updated R1 reasoner with open weights. Emits explicit chain-of-thought and remains a favorite for math and verification tasks on a budget.

name: Meta: Llama 4 Maverick
description: Meta's larger Llama 4 MoE with native image input. Open weights served by several fast hosts; a solid open-model default for tool-using apps.

name: Meta: Llama 4 Scout
description: The small Llama 4 with an extreme ten-million-token window. Open weights; useful for long-document work where price beats polish.

name: Meta: Llama 3.3 70B Instruct
description: The most deployed open dense model of its generation. Predictable, well-tooled, and available from many providers at commodity prices.

name: Meta: Llama 3.1 8B Instruct
description: A small open model that punches above its size for extraction and routing tasks. Nearly free at scale.

name: Mistral: Mistral Large 3 2512
description: Mistral's third-generation Large. European-hosted frontier-class generalist with vision and clean function calling.

name: Mistral: Mistral Medium 3.5
description: The current Mistral Medium: most of Large's quality with reasoning support at a mid price. The default Mistral pick.

name: Mistral: Mistral Small 4
description: Mistral Small 4. Compact, quick, vision-capable, and cheap enough for high-volume product use.

name: Mistral: Codestral 2508
description: Mistral's code specialist. Fill-in-the-middle and long-context repository awareness, tuned for IDE latency.

name: Qwen: Qwen3.7 Max
description: Alibaba's flagship Qwen. Frontier-class reasoning and multilingual strength over a million-token window, API-only.

name: Qwen: Qwen3.6 Plus
description: The balanced Qwen tier with text, image, and video input. Strong quality per dollar for multilingual and agentic work.

name: Qwen: Qwen3.5-27B
description: An open-weight multimodal Qwen that fits on a single node. Popular self-host choice with wide host coverage.

name: xAI: Grok 4.3
description: xAI's current mainline model. Sharp reasoning, live-web awareness through its native stack, and a million-token window.

name: xAI: Grok 4.20
description: The long-context Grok, stretching to two million tokens. Same pricing as 4.3; pick it for giant single-shot contexts.

name: MoonshotAI: Kimi K2.6
description: Moonshot's newest K2. A trillion-parameter-class MoE with vision, strong agentic coding, and open weights on several hosts.

name: MoonshotAI: Kimi K2 Thinking
description: The reasoning variant of Kimi K2. Long, explicit thinking traces and durable multi-step tool use; open weights.

name: Z.ai: GLM 5
description: Zhipu's GLM 5 flagship. Competitive coding and agent performance with open weights and very low hosted prices.

name: Z.ai: GLM 4.7
description: The prior GLM generation, still a bargain for coding assistants and bulk generation.

name: MiniMax: MiniMax M3
description: MiniMax's M3 MoE with text, image, and video input and a half-million-token output ceiling. Priced to undercut everything comparable.

name: NVIDIA: Nemotron 3 Super
description: NVIDIA's open Nemotron 3 Super, a sparse 120B tuned for agentic reasoning. Cheap hosted capacity and permissive licensing.

name: Tsuji: Fusion
description: A panel of frontier models answers your prompt in parallel, a judge compares the answers for agreement and contradiction, and a writer composes the final response from the panel plus the judge's notes. Billed as the sum of the underlying calls; listed prices are zero because cost depends on the panel.

name: Tsuji: Fusion Budget
description: The fusion pipeline on a panel of inexpensive models. Same panel, judge, and writer flow as tsuji/fusion, tuned so the summed cost stays close to a single frontier-model call.

name: Tsuji: Fusion Fast
description: The fusion pipeline on a small panel of low-latency models. Trades panel breadth for speed; the writer starts sooner because the slowest panel member is still fast.
